title: Reflected XSS via Unescaped REQUEST_URI in Admin Review Notice Links
description: The function wccp_free_review_notice_message() (notifications.php:121) constructs dismiss and 'remind later' URLs by directly concatenating $_SERVER['REQUEST_URI'] without URL-safe HTML escaping, then echoes them into href attributes of anchor tags without applying esc_url(). WordPress's wp_nonce_url() only appends a nonce query parameter — it does not HTML-encode the URL for safe embedding in an href context. This creates a reflected XSS vulnerability in the WordPress admin panel.

taint_source.note: wp_parse_url() is only used to check if query string exists (to pick '?' vs '&'); the raw REQUEST_URI is used directly at lines 124 and 127

missing_sanitizer: esc_url() must be applied to all URL outputs in href attributes. Additionally, $_SERVER['REQUEST_URI'] should be sanitized before concatenation.

exploit_scenario.summary: Attacker tricks admin into clicking a crafted URL containing a malicious payload in the URI path or query string.
prerequisites: Plugin must have been active for more than 5 days (432000 seconds) — OR developer mode enabled
Admin has not previously dismissed the review notice
Victim must be an authenticated WordPress admin (manage_options capability)
Victim must be induced to navigate to a crafted admin URL
steps: 1. Attacker crafts a URL to a WordPress admin page with a malicious REQUEST_URI, e.g.: https://victim.example.com/wp-admin/admin.php?page=any%22%20onmouseover%3D%22alert(document.cookie)
2. Attacker sends this URL to the admin via phishing, support link, etc.
3. Admin opens the URL while logged in; the review notice is shown (if conditions met)
4. The href attribute of the 'dismiss' / 'remind later' links contains the injected payload
5. Admin interaction (mouseover, click) triggers JavaScript execution in admin context
6. Attacker can steal admin cookies, perform CSRF-bypassing actions, escalate privileges

impact.confidentiality: low-medium — admin session cookies/tokens could be stolen
impact.integrity: low-medium — attacker can perform admin actions via injected JS
impact.scope: changed — JS executes in admin browser origin

nonce_context: Nonce is verified for the dismiss/later ACTION (prevents CSRF on the dismissal operation itself), but does not protect against XSS in the URL output

remediation.primary_fix:
Apply esc_url() to all echoed URLs in href attributes:
  Line 144: <a href="<?php echo esc_url($dismiss_url); ?>">
  Line 145: <a href="<?php echo esc_url($later_url); ?>">
  Line 146: <a href="<?php echo esc_url($dismiss_url); ?>">

Also sanitize the base URL before building dismiss/later URLs:
  $base_url = esc_url_raw($_SERVER['REQUEST_URI']);
  $url = $base_url . $scheme . 'wccp_free_review_dismiss=yes';
remediation.defense_in_depth: Consider using wp_safe_redirect() pattern or building URLs from known-safe components rather than directly using REQUEST_URI.

references: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation
